pi_items.source: Matches verifier.py hardcoded logic. However, direct extraction of Brasilia's WKB geometry from the world_capitals table reveals the actual map marker is at lat -15.7801, lon -47.9297.
verifier.py SQL query filtering for field_observations names.
Confirmed by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh WORK_GPKG paths.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating points around the capital Brasilia. The target location coordinates in the verifier are hardcoded to lat -15.793889, lon -47.882778. However, direct inspection of the provided world_survey.gpkg database reveals that the actual point marker for Brasilia is located at lat -15.7801, lon -47.9297. Because the verifier strictly requires the drawn points to be within ~0.0045 degrees (500 meters) of its hardcoded coordinates, and the actual map marker is ~0.0489 degrees (~5.4 km) away from that location, an agent that correctly centers its points around the visual map marker will perpetually fail the verifier distance check. The exact max allowed distance is 500 / 111320 (~0.00449) degrees, contradicting the metadata claim of 0.005.
